frontier: Erdős #979: A385316 — find a(6), the smallest N representable as p³+q³+r³ (p,q,r primes, nondecreasing) in exactly 6 ways. Current best bound: a(6) > 499,243,435,237 (~4.99×10¹¹). Exact terms a(1)–a(5) = 24, 185527, 8627527, 999979163, 10588881419.

The next gate requires executing C=10¹³ bounded exact search, but DGX SSH infrastructure (both dgx DNS resolution and spark-38e3.local key auth) remains unreachable — the blocker from the accepted continuation is unchanged. Local execution of C=10¹³ is estimated at ~20 minutes CPU time with ~50–70GB RAM, exceeding the 24GB available on this machine; C=10¹² would be feasible locally (~117s, ~10GB) but is a different cutoff than the gate.
